title: Token-bucket rate limiter middleware
instruction: Implement a token-bucket rate limiter as Express-style middleware: configurable capacity and refill rate per key (default key = client IP), returning HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header when exhausted. Refill must be time-based (not per-request). Include unit tests for allow, deny, and refill-over-time behavior using a fake clock.

criteria.guidance: Is refill time-based and are allow/deny/429/Retry-After correct?
Is the bucket state encapsulated and free of race-prone shortcuts?
Is per-request work O(1) with no unbounded memory growth?
